Bus Tour Day 4 — Kumano Sanzan

Sunday, May 24, 2026  •  Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus  •  Yunomine Onsen → Kii-Katsuura
Morning — Yunomine Onsen
Walk
Minshuku Takiyoshi → Yunomine Onsen Bus Stop Depart inn by 9:30 AM — bus departs at 9:44 AM sharp
🕑 Timing
Leave Bags by: 9:30 AM Walk: ~5 min / ~300 m Bus Departs: 9:44 AM
🚌 Walking Directions to Bus Stop
  • Exit Minshuku Takiyoshi — turn right, heading uphill (north)
  • Follow the main village path along the hot spring creek, passing Tsuboyu (壺湯) on your right
  • Continue uphill ~200 m to the junction with Route 311 (the main road)
  • Turn right onto Route 311 — the Yunomine Onsen bus stop (covered shelter with timetable) is immediately on your right
📱 Navigation Search “Yunomine Onsen Bus Stop” in Google Maps — or look for the blue bus shelter at the Route 311 junction at the top of the village.

Yunomine Onsen (湯の峰温泉) — follow the creek path uphill to the Route 311 bus stop · CC BY-SA 4.0 Nekosuki

⚠ Board at the MAIN “Yunomine Onsen” Stop Only Do NOT board at Shimo-yunomine (下湯の峰) — that is a different stop further down the route. Wait at the main Yunomine Onsen stop (blue shelter at the Route 311 junction at the top of the village).
📦 Luggage — Forwarded Hands-Free Leave 2 bags at Minshuku Takiyoshi by 9:30 AM. The Maruo luggage shuttle will collect and deliver directly to KAMENOI HOTEL Nachi-Katsuura. Do not carry bags on the bus.
Kumano Sanzan Bus Tour
Bus Tour
Kumano Sanzan — Yunomine Onsen to Kii-Katsuura Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus — blue/white coach — ~¥12,600/person
🊭 Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus (blue/white coach) — Seats are coach-style. Board hands-free — luggage forwarded separately to hotel.
⚠ Confirm if pre-paid — if ticket not issued in advance, pay by card at boarding.

Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus (Bus #806) — cream/white coach with red & gold KUMANO livery · © Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus Co.

Stamp Red circle = goshuuin (御朱印) stamp available at this stop — bring your stamp book (shuinchō).
  • 1
    Yunomine Onsen 湯の峰温泉
    9:44 AM — Boarding
    Board the Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus at the main stop. Show booking confirmation or ticket. Departs promptly — be at the stop a few minutes early.
  • 2
    Kumano Hongu Taisha 熊野本宮大社
    ~10:00 AM — 40 min stop
    First of the three Grand Shrines of Kumano. Enter through the towering Otorii gate and climb the stone steps to the main worship halls (honden). The complex dates to the 9th century.
    🆕 Goshuuin (御朱印) — Stamp Locations
    1. Main Shrine Office — Inside the complex, past the Otorii gate and up the stone stairs, on the left. Typically ¥500. May have a short queue.
    2. Kumano Hongu Heritage Center (World Heritage Stamp) — Just outside the main gate, left side. Free stamp — get this if time allows after the shrine.
    Strategy: Go to shrine office first, then Heritage Center on the way back to the bus.

    Kumano Hongu Heritage Center (熊野本宮館) — World Heritage visitor center just outside the main gate; free World Heritage stamp available inside · CC BY-SA 4.0

    🚌 Re-board by 10:40 AM Return to bus at Hongu Taisha bus stop
  • 3
    Kumano Hayatama Taisha 熊野速玉大社
    ~12:20 PM — 20 min stop
    Second Grand Shrine, located in Shingu City on the Kumano River. Famous for vivid vermilion shrine buildings. A nagi tree (sacred to Kumano pilgrims) over 1,000 years old stands in the precinct.
    🆕 Goshuuin (御朱印)
    Shrine office (shamusho) on the right side of the main path, after passing through the entrance torii. Typically ¥500.
    Only 20 minutes — go directly to the stamp office first, then pay respects at the main hall on the way back.

    Kumano Hayatama Taisha (熊野速玉大社) entrance — stamp office is on the right past the torii · CC BY-SA 4.0

    🚌 Re-board by 12:40 PM Return to bus at Hayatama Taisha stop
  • 4
    Nachi Waterfall 那智の滝
    ~1:30 PM — 25–30 min stop
    Japan’s tallest single-drop waterfall at 133 m. A stone path leads to the viewing platform below the falls with an iconic torii gate framing the view. Entrance fee ~¥300 to the inner viewing area.
    🆕 Stamp / Goshuuin
    Hirou Jinja (飛瀧神社) — the Shinto shrine at the base of the falls, within the viewing area. Stamp available inside.
    A World Heritage stamp is also available at the main info booth near the viewing platform entrance.

    Nachi-no-Taki (那智の滝) — Japan's tallest single-drop waterfall at 133 m · CC BY 3.0

    🚌 Re-board by 1:55 PM Bus drives up to Nachi Taisha entrance (~5 min)
  • 5
    Kumano Nachi Taisha 熊野那智大社
    ~2:00 PM — 50–60 min stop
    Third Grand Shrine, set in the mountains above the waterfall. Adjacent to Seiganto-ji (青岸渡寺), a Buddhist temple — a rare surviving Shinto-Buddhist complex. The iconic three-storey pagoda overlooks the Nachi Falls below and is one of Japan’s most photographed views.
    🆕 Two Stamp Opportunities
    1. Kumano Nachi Taisha — Shrine stamp office inside the main compound. ¥500.
    2. Seiganto-ji (青岸渡寺) — Temple stamp office immediately next door (30 sec walk). ¥300–¥500.
    Both are side-by-side — stamp at Nachi Taisha first, then Seiganto-ji. Allow ~10 min total for both queues.

    Seiganto-ji three-storey pagoda (青岸渡寺) framing Nachi Falls — Kumano's most iconic view · CC BY-SA 4.0

    🚌 Re-board by 2:50 PM ~20 min drive to Kii-Katsuura — must depart by 2:50 PM to arrive 3:10 PM
  • 6
    Kii-Katsuura Station 紀伊勝浦駅
    3:10 PM — End of Tour
    Tour concludes at Kii-Katsuura Station. Collect all items from overhead storage. Walk to KAMENOI HOTEL — directions in the next section below.

    Kii-Katsuura Station (紀伊勝浦駅) — exit south toward the harbor for Kamenoi Hotel · CC BY-SA 4.0

Arrival — Kii-Katsuura
Walk
Kii-Katsuura Station → KAMENOI HOTEL Nachi-Katsuura ~7 min walk — ~550 m — near the tourist pier / harbor
🕑 Timing
Arrive Station: 3:10 PM Walk: ~7 min / ~550 m Check-in By: 18:00
🏘 Kii-Katsuura Station → KAMENOI HOTEL
  • Exit Kii-Katsuura Station from the main (south / sea-facing) exit
  • Walk south along the main street toward the ocean and harbor
  • Follow signs for “港” (Port / Harbor) or “Kamenoi Hotel”
  • The hotel entrance is near the Katsuura Tourist Pier on the right as you reach the waterfront — look for the hotel signage facing the bay
📱 Navigation Search “Kamenoi Hotel Nachi Katsuura” or “亀の井ホテル 那智勝浦” in Google Maps / Apple Maps.
Check-in
KAMENOI HOTEL Nachi-Katsuura — Check-in & Evening Tasks Japanese-style bed • Ocean View • Supper & Breakfast included • Kumano Travel Res #186433
🕐 Check-in by 18:00 — Meals: Supper & Breakfast included.
Luggage: 2 bags arriving from Minshuku Takiyoshi via Maruo luggage shuttle — confirm at reception on arrival.

KAMENOI HOTEL Nachi-Katsuura — main entrance from the arrival forecourt; hotel sign and covered drop-off visible · © Kamenoi Hotels

Private open-air onsen (露天風呂) with direct bay views — available for in-room or shared bath reservations · © Kamenoi Hotels

  • 1
    Pay Onsen Tax at reception
    ¥150/person × 2 = ¥300 total — payable directly to hotel at check-in. Cash only.
  • 2
    Arrange Takkyubin pickup for May 25 AM
    Ask front desk to arrange luggage forwarding for tomorrow morning.
    → Destination: Hotel Trusty Osaka Abeno, Tennoji
    → 2 bags — drop at front desk by 9:00 AM on May 25 (Monday)
    → ~¥2,510/bag — cash only
💴 Cash to Prepare at Check-in
Onsen Tax (¥150 × 2 persons)¥300
Takkyubin — 2 bags to Hotel Trusty Osaka Abeno¥5,020
Total Cash Needed¥5,320
Reservation: Kumano Travel Res #186433 • Hotel: KAMENOI HOTEL Nachi-Katsuura • Night: May 24–25, 2026 • Check-in by 18:00
